exemplar.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, focused engagement with the title across Natural Sciences and the Arts
Clear, coherent arguments supported by well-chosen examples (rainbows, Milky Way, Manet)
Awareness and balanced evaluation of alternative perspectives (Weberâs disenchantment vs. knowledge deepening wonder)
Personal anecdotes and scholarly references that lend authenticity and coherence
weaknesses: Evaluation of implications remains brief and could be deepened into systematic analysis
Some examples are treated in broad strokes without probing underlying knowledge questions
Structural redundancies and occasional orphaned or fragmented sentences disrupt flow
Citation and referencing inconsistencies (missing in-text citations, secondary sources, formatting errors)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay clearly meets the descriptors for band 7â8: it maintains a sustained focus on the prescribed title, makes effective links to two relevant Areas of Knowledge, and presents clear, coherent arguments supported by specific examples. There is evidence of evaluative awareness of different viewpoints and consideration of implications, though not to the depth required for the top band. Structural and citation weaknesses, along with occasional under-developed analysis, prevent the work from reaching band 9â10. On balance, an 8 out of 10 reflects its strong performance within band 7â8.

annotations.comment: The description of infrared telescopes is informative, but the essay misses the opportunity to pose a knowledge question (e.g., How do tools shape our wonder?). Framing discoveries as questions deepens TOK analysis.
The personal reflection âI, myself, grow more curiousâ¦â adds authenticity but could be followed by evaluation of potential limitations, ensuring the reflection remains analytical.
The opening of the Arts section repeats earlier claims about deepening wonder. To maintain coherence and avoid redundancy, streamline this transition with a concise linking sentence.
This standalone sentence appears orphaned. Integrate it fully into the preceding or following paragraph to preserve structural flow.
The Manet example is well chosen and personally relevant. It effectively illustrates how contextual knowledge can enrich engagement with art rather than diminish it.
To strengthen this example, discuss specific reactions or critical responses to Manetâs Olympia, linking the viewerâs emotional shift to the TOK concept of value judgments.
The Cezanne quote is powerful but lacks an in-text citation and full reference in the bibliography. IB essays require precise attribution of all quoted material.
The poetry analysis remains abstract without naming a poem or quoting lines. Including a specific text will ground the argument and demonstrate concrete engagement.

The introduction restates the title clearly but would benefit from a more precise articulation of the central knowledge question and a sharper thesis. Explicitly framing the inquiry in TOK terms will guide the essayâs argument more coherently.
Clear identification of Natural Sciences and The Arts demonstrates focus on two relevant AOKs. This provides a strong foundation for structured analysis throughout the essay.

The magic-trick analogy is engaging but overly elaborate. Trimming this section and linking it more directly to TOK conceptsâsuch as the role of explanation in shaping perceptionâwould strengthen the introduction.
The definition of âsense of wonderâ is stated, but the essay would benefit from distinguishing this concept from related TOK ideas like emotion and aesthetics to deepen the theoretical framework.

The provisional thesisâthat knowledge either erodes or redefines wonderâcould be formulated more directly. A concise thesis statement will help readers anticipate the essayâs structure and arguments.

In the rainbow example, the source (BYJUâS) is a secondary educational site. For TOK, prioritize more authoritative scientific sources or scholarly articles to evaluate the reliability and credibility of evidence.
Watch for typographical errors like âwe know knowâ and âinfact.â Such mistakes distract from the argument and undermine the essayâs academic tone.

The discussion of Max Weberâs concept of disenchantment misattributes his name as âWebber.â Ensure accurate representation of key theorists and deepen the analysis of how disenchantment functions as a knowledge framework.
Link the disenchantment example explicitly to a TOK knowledge questionâsuch as the interplay between explanation and emotional responseâto reinforce the essayâs conceptual coherence.
The transition to a counter-perspective on scientific discovery enhancing curiosity is effective. This balanced treatment shows awareness of different points of view.
The Milky Way example lacks specific historical context (e.g., the date of Galileoâs observations). Including precise timelines and primary sources would add academic rigour.
The topic sentence could be sharpened by explicitly stating the knowledge question guiding the exploration of the Arts, rather than a general claim about appreciation.
This fragmentary sentence would read more fluently if merged into the preceding paragraph. Ensure each sentence advances the essayâs analytical momentum.
Citation formatting varies across entries. Adopt a single style (e.g., MLA or APA) consistently, including author names, titles, dates, and access information.
BYJUâS is a popular educational site but not a primary academic source. Replace or supplement it with peer-reviewed literature or reputable scientific publications to enhance credibility.
Several entries lack DOIs or standardized access dates. Including complete publication details for each source will meet IB requirements and improve traceability.
The MIT Press Reader entry is suitable, but consider adding scholarly analysis or journal articles to balance popular and academic sources in your bibliography.
The discussion of emotion and language as Ways of Knowing is pertinent; consider expanding on how these WOKs interact to reshape our experience of wonder in both AOKs.

The conclusion restates the cycle of curiosity but doesnât explicitly revisit the core knowledge question or its implications. Tie the closing remark back to the TOK question to solidify your argument.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
